signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.86, well above the 160.00 intervention watch level. Reduced tanker flows through the Strait of Hormuz (WTI Oil $70.4, +1.72%) and Iran's President's 'unfounded threats' rhetoric escalate geopolitical tensions. JPY COT LF net short −115,033 (28.4% long) is an extreme crowded short, while the USD/JPY OIS carry of +266bp continues to support the pair. Intervention risk is elevated on sustained price action above 160.00, with a crowded short vulnerable to a BoJ-induced squeeze. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines the yield gap direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkishness vs Political Risk + Positioning
signals.text: ECB’s Lagarde states the ECB can raise rates to address inflation without fear, signaling hawkish intent despite HSBC noting political and growth risks pointing lower for EUR/USD. EUR COT LF net short −67,504 (34.5% long) indicates significant speculative short positioning. EUR/USD OIS carry is +146bp favoring USD, structurally limiting EUR upside. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul is key for rate guidance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Volatility Premium
signals.text: AUD is the weakest G10 currency this week with an average of −0.438% vs peers, while EUR is the strongest at +0.437%, creating a 0.875% spread. AUD/USD IV at 23.0% vs HV30 at 7.5% shows an extreme +15.5% VRP, indicating strong hedging demand for AUD downside protection. This suggests options traders are paying up for AUD weakness despite neutral speculative positioning (AUD COT LF net long +29,035). The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug is the catalyst.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Extreme Volatility Premium
signals.text: USD/CAD retail sentiment is extremely short at 94% short, diverging from COT data which shows CAD NET SHORT, lev_net=-87,096 (20.6% long), indicating institutional short positioning. USD/CAD IV at 13.8% vs HV30 at 6.4% shows a +7.4% VRP, signaling elevated options market demand for protection. This retail extreme against institutional positioning and high vol premium suggests potential for a short squeeze if CAD data disappoints. Watch for the next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 Jul.
